channel.title: INmaster
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from INmaster, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Deal of the day: Fly to Jamaica for only 8,000 American Airlines AAdvantage miles
item.description: Ready for some fun in the Caribbean sun? You can visit Jamaica for less with this deal.

item.title: How to redeem American Airlines AAdvantage miles for maximum value
item.description: A look at the best sweet spot redemptions in the American Airlines AAdvantage program.

item.title: Chase Sapphire Reserve vs. Amex Gold: A top luxury travel card against a popular card for foodies
item.description: The American Express Gold is one of the only mid-tier cards that can give the Chase Sapphire Reserve a run for its money. Here's how they stack up.

item.title: Antarctica reading list: The 8 best books to read before a trip
item.description: It pays to do your homework before a once-in-a-lifetime trip to Antarctica

item.title: Points and miles aren’t just for the good times. They can be a godsend in an emergency, too
item.description: When a family emergency struck thousands of miles from home, points and miles saved me and my husband.

item.title: Crack Chicken Tacos
item.description: Get ready to be instantly obsessed with these crack chicken tacos! They are loaded with creamy ranch chicken, crispy bacon,…

item.title: Mysterious ‘Hero’ Dog Leads Police Straight to Missing 3-year-old Officer Says in Body Cam Video
item.description: A police officer and self-described “dog guy,” hooked his own instincts up to those of a local dog to find a missing 3-year-old in Kentucky. There was...

item.title: The Best Noise-Canceling Headphones for Traveling Are $50 Off
item.description: With excellent noise canceling, the QuietComfort Ultra 2 would love to join you on your next long-haul flight.

item.title: Climactic launches hybrid fund to get startups through the ‘valley of death’
item.description: The new project, called Material Scale, will initially focus on climate tech startups in the apparel industry.

item.title: Apple is reportedly cooking up a trio of AI wearables
item.description: As the AI hardware space heats up, the iPhone maker has multiple smart products in development.

item.title: Volunteers Save 51 Dogs in Large-Scale Operation Following Arkansas Shelter Collapse
item.description: From Arkansas comes the story of a huge animal rescue operation of 51 dogs trapped in a derelict shelter. Headquartered in neighboring Nashville, the ...

item.title: Thrive raises $10B for new fund, its largest yet
item.description: Thrive Capital just raised $10 billion for its new fund-- nearly double the size of its last fund.

item.title: ‘Mushroom Mining’ Could Be Cheap Way to Recover Rare Earth Minerals from Industrial Waste
item.description: Decades of underinvestment in mining and refining across Europe and North America has landed the manufacturing sector in a problem, which a group of s...

item.title: Meta’s own research found parental supervision doesn’t really help curb teens’ compulsive social media use
item.description: An internal research study at Meta found that parental supervision may not help teens regulate their social media, and teens with trauma are more incl...

item.title: Anthropic releases Sonnet 4.6
item.description: Anthropic has released a new version of its mid-size Sonnet model, keeping pace with the company's four-month update cycle.

item.title: Good News in History, February 17
item.description: Happy 69th Birthday to a titan of folk music: the multi-instrumentalist Loreena McKennitt. Born in Manitoba to Irish-Scottish ancestry, she fell in lo...

item.title: Inside the Homeland Security Forum Where ICE Agents Talk Shit About Other Agents
item.description: Forum members have discussed their discomfort with mass deportation efforts, debate how federal agents have interacted with civilians, and complain ab...

item.title: Meta and Other Tech Companies Ban OpenClaw Over Cybersecurity Concerns
item.description: Security experts have urged people to be cautious with the viral agentic AI tool, known for being highly capable but also wildly unpredictable.

item.title: The Curling Controversy at the Winter Olympics Isn’t What You Think
item.description: After Swedish curler Oskar Eriksson accused Canadian vice-skip Marc Kennedy of cheating last week, everyone has become an expert in curling rules. The...

item.title: Urban Farm Shops Combine Dutch Greenhouse Technology and Food-Retail into One
item.description: In the city of The Hague, a Dutch agri-tech firm has had the bright idea of combining a greenhouse grow operation with a grocery and delivery business...

item.title: The Simplest Android App for Scanning Documents
item.description: Most scanning apps try to get you to buy a cloud storage subscription or pay for extras. Not FairScan, which is free and open-source, and has some pow...

item.title: Starbucks Copycat Chocolate Pistachio Loaf
item.description: Starbucks dropped a new Chocolate Pistachio Loaf, and I was instantly obsessed. The pistachio flavor and that rich chocolate swirl…

item.title: What Happens When Blood Sugar Falls Too Low
7 Nutrition Red Flags to Know About
item.description: Feeling unusually tired or noticing subtle changes in hair, nails, mood, or appetite? These signs may indicate nutrition gaps worth discussing with yo...

item.title: Crockpot Chicken Marsala
item.description: This crockpot chicken marsala brings restaurant-style flavor straight to your slow cooker. Tender chicken, mushrooms, and a rich, savory sauce…

item.title: Tips for Making Exercise a Daily Habit

item.description: This horseradish pot roast is pure comfort food with a flavorful twist. The beef cooks low and slow until fork-tender,…

item.title: Weekly Meal Plan #77
item.description: If the thought of figuring out dinner every night is weighing you down, this week’s meal plan is for you.…

item.title: Fudgy Chocolate Tahini Cookies (1 Bowl!)

item.title: Vegan Gluten-Free Morning Glory Muffins
item.description: Say hello to morning glory muffins, made vegan + gluten-free ! A classic treat originally created by Pam McKinstry in 1978 at her Nantucket restaurant...

item.title: 63 Essential Things to Know Before Visiting Tokyo
item.description: From trains to table manners, these are the lessons Tokyo quietly teaches travellers. They’re practical, cultural, and often invisible until you get t...

item.title: What grocery stores reveal about how people eat
item.description: A grocery store is one of the most revealing places you can walk into when travelling. More than restaurants or cafés, it shows how people actually ea...

item.title: What is considered a “normal meal” in different cultures
item.description: People often search for a “normal meal” when they arrive somewhere new. Something familiar enough to feel grounding, but local enough to feel authenti...

item.title: Why lunch is the main meal in some countries
item.description: For many travellers, dinner feels like the main event. It is where expectations concentrate: the moment reserved for something special, social, or mem...

item.title: Creamy Curried Black Eyed Pea Soup

item.title: Tahini Hot Chocolate (Super Dreamy, Protein-Rich!)
item.description: If you’re looking for a decadent, elevated hot chocolate with wholesome ingredients, this one’s for you! We’ve been serving up this frothy, tahini-inf...

item.title: Jobless claims fall to lowest level since mid-May
Jobless claims stay low in latest week
Consumer credit growth soars in December
U.S. productivity slows down in fourth quarter while unit labor costs accelerate
Beyond to buy rights to Buy Buy Baby brand and reunite it with Bed Bath & Beyond
